name: Matters promotional visual profile

usage: Use purple and lime as the strongest brand signal.
Use black or white as the main text surface.
Keep legacy green or gold only for archived references unless a source file explicitly requires it.

notes: Prefer Noto Sans TC for labels, metadata, buttons, and UI text.
Use Noto Serif TC for editorial headlines, quotes, and literary campaign tone.
Do not ask the image model to render final Chinese text; render text through templates.

principles: Strong editorial focal point with generous breathing room.
Use deterministic text-safe areas for headlines and CTAs.
Prefer one hero idea over many decorative elements.
Brand mark should be applied by template, not generated into the image.

safeAreas.square: Reserve at least 30% of the canvas for text overlay.
safeAreas.landscape: Reserve either left 42% or right 42% for headline and metadata.

imageGeneration.accessNote: gpt-image-2 may require OpenAI Organization verification before the API accepts requests.
backgroundRules: Avoid fake UI, fake logos, fake app screenshots, QR codes, and unreadable pseudo text.
Leave clear negative space for overlaid copy.
Use visual metaphors related to writing, publishing, reading, public conversation, archive, or digital commons.

reviewChecklist: Does the result still read as Matters without relying on random purple-green decoration?
Is all visible text rendered by HTML/CSS, not by the image model?
Is there enough contrast for Chinese text?
Can the same job be rerun with a different title without reworking the layout?
